LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BARBARA GREGORATOS AND JEFFREY BRANDSTETTER ANTI-SLAPP MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED, DEFENDANTS FAIL TO SHOW THAT THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARISE FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY. WHILE THE SERVICE OF A NOTICE TO TERMINATE MAY BE PRIVILEGED, THE SERVICE WAS NOT THE CAUSE OF PLAINTIFFS' CAUSES OF ACTION. THE GRAVAMEN OF PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS THE DEFENDANTS' FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE PROVISIONS OF THE SAN FRANCISCO RENT ORDINANCE. SEE MARLIN VS. AMICO VENEZIA LLC (2007) 154 CAL.APP. 4TH 154 AND CLARK VS. MAZGANI (2009) 170 CAL.APP. 4TH 1281. INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE PRESENTED THAT DEFENDANTS' MOTION WAS FRIVOLOUS OR SOLELY INTENDED TO CAUSE UNNECESSARY DELAY. REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY FEES DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-08-2010 Continued To Aug-20-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Notice Of Deft'S Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike Complaint; Request For Judicial Notice Of Deft'S Anti-Slapp
Off Calendar Mar-25-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS BARBARA GREGORATOS, and, JEFFREY BRANDSTETTER Notice of Defendant's Anti-SLAPP Motion to Strike Complaint. Appearances: Jonathan Joiner, Esq. Law Offices of Richard Hurlburt (415) 391-6496 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; David Semel, Esq. of Haapala, Altura, Thompson & Abern LLP (510) 763-2324 appearing for Defendants and Moving party; Jeffrey D. Brandstetter, Defendant (415) 571-8600 also appearing. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied, defendants fail to show that the causes of action arise from protected activity. While the service of a notice to terminate may be privileged, the service was not the cause of plaintiffs' causes of action. The gravamen of plaintiffs' complaint is the defendants' failure to comply with the provisions of the San Francisco Rent Ordinance. See Marlin vs. Amico Venezia LLC (2007) 154 Cal.App. 4th 154 and Clark vs. Mazgani (2009) 170 Cal.App. 4th 1281. Insufficient evidence presented that defendants' motion was frivolous or solely intended to cause unnecessary delay. Request for attorney fees denied. Order signed in open Court. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
